channel.title: yourfdc.au News — Family Day Care Australia
channel.description: Australia's dedicated Family Day Care news hub.

item.title: A trainee's perspective: who is supporting the people we are asking to stay?
item.description: When we talk about workforce shortages in early childhood education and care, we often discuss recruitment, qualifications and retention. But from a trainee's perspective, there is another conversation we need to have.
item.content:encoded: When we talk about workforce shortages in early childhood education and care, we often discuss recruitment, qualifications and retention. But from a trainee's perspective, there is another conversation we need to have.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Private investment continues to back childcare despite sector headwinds
item.description: Recent market activity has highlighted an emerging divergence in Australia's early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector. While publicly listed providers continue to navigate rising costs, regulatory reform and margin pressure, private investors appear to remain confident in the sector's long-term fundamentals.
item.content:encoded: Recent market activity has highlighted an emerging divergence in Australia's early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector. While publicly listed providers continue to navigate rising costs, regulatory reform and margin pressure, private investors appear to remain confident in the sector's long-term fundamentals.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Mayfield Childcare withdraws FY26 guidance as sector pressures intensify
item.description: Mayfield Childcare Limited has withdrawn its FY26 earnings guidance, citing a rapidly evolving operating environment across the early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector. The ASX-listed provider, which operates 45 centres across Victoria, Queensland and South Australia, said a combination of regulatory change, rising labour costs and occupancy volatility had materially affected the assumptions underpinning its previously issued outlook.
item.content:encoded: Mayfield Childcare Limited has withdrawn its FY26 earnings guidance, citing a rapidly evolving operating environment across the early childhood education and care (ECEC) sector. The ASX-listed provider, which operates 45 centres across Victoria, Queensland and South Australia, said a combination of regulatory change, rising labour costs and occupancy volatility had materially affected the assumptions underpinning its previously issued outlook.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: NT Children's Commissioner resigns amid concerns over governance and child protection reforms
item.description: The resignation of Northern Territory Children's Commissioner Shahleena Musk has intensified scrutiny of the Territory Government's child protection reform agenda, with child advocacy organisations, Aboriginal leaders and legal experts raising concerns about governance, transparency and independent oversight.
item.content:encoded: The resignation of Northern Territory Children's Commissioner Shahleena Musk has intensified scrutiny of the Territory Government's child protection reform agenda, with child advocacy organisations, Aboriginal leaders and legal experts raising concerns about governance, transparency and independent oversight.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Regional Education Commissioner outlines roadmap for improving ECEC access in regional Australia
item.description: A universal early childhood education and care (ECEC) service obligation, flexible delivery models and sustainable funding for services operating in thin markets are among the key recommendations outlined in the Regional Education Commissioner Annual Report 2025, which argues that improving access to ECEC is fundamental to the future of regional Australia.
item.content:encoded: A universal early childhood education and care (ECEC) service obligation, flexible delivery models and sustainable funding for services operating in thin markets are among the key recommendations outlined in the Regional Education Commissioner Annual Report 2025, which argues that improving access to ECEC is fundamental to the future of regional Australia.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Educator Essentials: new Queensland resources support positive behaviour guidance in early learning
item.description: Creating learning environments where children feel safe, respected and supported is central to high-quality early childhood education and care. To help educators strengthen this aspect of practice, the Queensland Department of Education has released Educator Essentials—Positive Behaviour Strategies, a new video series offering practical, evidence-informed guidance for educators working with children aged three to five years.
item.content:encoded: Creating learning environments where children feel safe, respected and supported is central to high-quality early childhood education and care. To help educators strengthen this aspect of practice, the Queensland Department of Education has released Educator Essentials—Positive Behaviour Strategies, a new video series offering practical, evidence-informed guidance for educators working with children aged three to five years.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: More than 120 families notified as Local Contact Point established in Sydney childcare worker investigation
item.description: Before continuing to read this piece, readers should be aware that the content of this article may prove distressing, and should consider their own circumstances before continuing to engage with the piece. A list of support services has been provided at the conclusion of the article.
item.content:encoded: Before continuing to read this piece, readers should be aware that the content of this article may prove distressing, and should consider their own circumstances before continuing to engage with the piece. A list of support services has been provided at the conclusion of the article.
item.dc:creator: The Sector

item.title: Everyone experiences loss and grief – but that doesn’t make it any easier to talk with kids about it
item.description: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.content:encoded: Parents and educators sometimes avoid having conversations about grief with children because they don’t know what to say. Here are some tips for how to approach it.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: The Breadwinner: Comedy doesn’t need flawless dads, but we do need competent ones
item.description: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.content:encoded: Comedic portrayals of incompetent dads can distort public understanding of fathers’ essential and real-life contributions to children’s development.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Childcare workers have been guaranteed a pay bump. What’s the fine print?
item.description: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.content:encoded: The federal government has set aside $3.6 billion over the next two years for early educator pay. But what happens after that is not yet clear.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Toy Story 5 pits traditional toys vs a tablet. In real life, families can combine the two
item.description: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.content:encoded: The latest instalment of the film franchise addresses a dilemma many parents face: what happens to playtime when children become obsessed with screens?
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: To improve children’s mental health, start by supporting their parents
item.description: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.content:encoded: Long-term data shows children are more likely to develop mental health issues when their parents experience financial stress and housing instability.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Is baby talk bad? Why ‘parentese’ actually helps babies learn language
item.description: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.content:encoded: Exaggerating phrases and talking in a sing-song way can actually help, not make it harder, for young children to master speaking a language.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Fixating on a ‘magic number’ of childcare hours misses what’s most important for kids’ development
item.description: A major Australian study found kids who spend 40 hours or more in childcare may struggle more with social skills and emotions than those who attend for less time.
item.content:encoded: A major Australian study found kids who spend 40 hours or more in childcare may struggle more with social skills and emotions than those who attend for less time.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Are you worried about your preschoolers’ anxiety? Here’s how to help
item.description: Research on a group of Australian preschoolers found more than 40% met the criteria for an anxiety disorder.
item.content:encoded: Research on a group of Australian preschoolers found more than 40% met the criteria for an anxiety disorder.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Trump’s new child care subsidy rules compound an already dire situation for providers and families
item.description: The Trump administration’s new child care subsidy rules are the latest measure to challenge the struggling industry.
item.content:encoded: The Trump administration’s new child care subsidy rules are the latest measure to challenge the struggling industry.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Why exposing young children to AI content could have irreversible consequences
item.description: Young children’s brains develop through interaction with others. Throwing AI into the mix could make it more difficult for them to navigate real-world relationships.
item.content:encoded: Young children’s brains develop through interaction with others. Throwing AI into the mix could make it more difficult for them to navigate real-world relationships.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Generative AI can play a role uplifting family and community in early childhood education
item.description: Researchers use text and image generators to design culturally relevant educational content for young children.
item.content:encoded: Researchers use text and image generators to design culturally relevant educational content for young children.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Good-quality child care? What parents should consider, and how it can be assessed
item.description: The quality of educator-child interactions is among the strongest predictors of children’s developmental and learning outcomes.
item.content:encoded: The quality of educator-child interactions is among the strongest predictors of children’s developmental and learning outcomes.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Colorado faces a funding crisis for child care − local communities hope to fill the gaps
item.description: A lack of access to child care costs the state an estimated $2.7 billion in lost productivity and revenue.
item.content:encoded: A lack of access to child care costs the state an estimated $2.7 billion in lost productivity and revenue.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Violence is a normal part of life for many young children: study traces the mental health impacts
item.description: Violence exposure in early childhood is widespread in low- and middle-income countries and has clear impacts on young children’s mental health.
item.content:encoded: Violence exposure in early childhood is widespread in low- and middle-income countries and has clear impacts on young children’s mental health.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Physio at 3 months old – or even earlier – can really help babies with cerebral palsy
item.description: Research has shown physio improves physical and cognitive outcomes for babies with cerebral palsy.
item.content:encoded: Research has shown physio improves physical and cognitive outcomes for babies with cerebral palsy.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: Naming and categorizing objects is part of how young kids develop executive function skills – new research
item.description: Kids with good executive function tend to grow up to be financially stable, healthy, productive adults. Past attempts to teach these skills have flopped. New brain research on language may change that.
item.content:encoded: Kids with good executive function tend to grow up to be financially stable, healthy, productive adults. Past attempts to teach these skills have flopped. New brain research on language may change that.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation

item.title: My child is always losing and forgetting things. How can I help – without making it worse?
item.description: Doing everything for your child robs them of an opportunity to learn. Here are the dos and don
item.content:encoded: Doing everything for your child robs them of an opportunity to learn. Here are the dos and don
item.dc:creator: The Conversation
